Here's a poem that captures the essence of my life as a model:

I pose in front of cameras, a perfect face,
But behind the scenes, my thoughts are in a frantic pace.
The pressure to look flawless, to fit the mold,
Leaves me feeling trapped, my true self growing old.

The glamour of the industry, it hides the pain,
Of being objectified, of losing my name.
I'm more than just a body, more than just a face,
But in this world of beauty, that's all I'm placed.

So I'll keep on smiling, keep on posing too,
But deep inside, I'm searching for a way to break through.
To be seen as more than just a pretty sight,
To find my true self, in the dark of night.
